Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Senior Accountant

Multiple Locations (San Francisco, New York, Toronto, Remote) • Full time • Hybrid/Remote

Cohere is at the forefront of AI innovation, building cutting-edge language models and AI systems. Our Finance team plays a critical role in supporting our rapid growth and ensuring operational excellence. We value technical expertise, collaborative problem-solving, and a commitment to accuracy and compliance. This role offers the opportunity to make a significant impact on our financial infrastructure while working at the intersection of finance and transformative technology.

We are seeking a Senior Accountant to support our General Ledger team with all processes and projects. This is an exciting opportunity to join a dynamic finance organization at a high-growth AI company where you can build accounting processes from the ground up in a fast-paced technology environment.

In this role, you will:

  • Support the GL team with comprehensive accounting processes including month-end close, reconciliations, and audit preparation

  • Assist with system implementations and process improvements for accounting infrastructure

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to build scalable accounting processes

  • Partner with external auditors and coordinate audit processes

  • Develop and maintain comprehensive accounting policies, procedures, and internal controls

  • Drive continuous improvement of accounting infrastructure and reporting capabilities

  • Take ownership of special projects including process optimization and automation initiatives

You may be a good fit if you:

  • Have a background in public accounting (Big 4 preferred) OR strong operational experience at a high-growth technology startup

  • Possess 5+ years of progressive accounting experience with increasing responsibility

  • Demonstrate experience with system implementations and process improvements

  • Show strong analytical skills and ability to perform complex reconciliations

  • Take a proactive, solutions-oriented approach to problem solving

  • Have a strong desire to build accounting processes in a fast-paced technology environment

Apply

If you are passionate about solving complex accounting challenges in a dynamic AI environment and want to join a team that values technical excellence and innovation, we encourage you to apply.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Technical Revenue Manager

Multiple Locations (San Francisco, New York, Toronto, Remote) • Full time • Hybrid/Remote

Cohere is at the forefront of AI innovation, building cutting-edge language models and AI systems. Our Finance team plays a critical role in supporting our rapid growth and ensuring operational excellence. We value technical expertise, collaborative problem-solving, and a commitment to accuracy and compliance. This role offers the opportunity to make a significant impact on our financial infrastructure while working at the intersection of finance and transformative technology. As we scale our business and navigate an evolving revenue landscape, we need a Technical Revenue Manager to own complex revenue technical positions for AI-native companies, ensuring robust revenue recognition frameworks that support our innovative product offerings and monetization strategies.

In this role, you will:

  • Own complex revenue technical positions for AI-native companies, focusing on high-impact revenue recognition projects that elevate our accounting and reporting capabilities

  • Analyze and implement accounting treatment for complex revenue transactions, including non-standard revenue contracts, modifications, new product offerings, credit-based pricing, and usage-based models

  • Review complex revenue arrangements to identify key terms and conditions that impact revenue recognition, deploying scalable accounting for these transactions

  • Design and implement systems and processes to automate revenue recognition in alignment with revenue recognition policies and objectives

  • Partner with the Deal Desk, Go-to-Market, and Strategic Finance teams to standardize and automate deal approvals, optimizing revenue recognition while supporting sales initiatives

  • Execute complex ASC 606 technical evaluations and draft comprehensive revenue accounting memos

  • Assist with the external audit process and participate in and/or lead ad hoc projects with cross-functional impacts

You may be a good fit if you:

  • Have 6+ years of experience in ASC 606 for SaaS, consumption-based, and professional services arrangements

  • Have 6+ years of experience in internal controls, SOX/ICFR, audit, financial process risk, or financial operations within high-growth, tech, or SaaS environments

  • Have proven experience advising Product and GTM teams on revenue recognition implications

  • Demonstrate strong contract review experience with multi-element arrangements, milestone-based SOWs, and credit/usage models

  • Have familiarity with order-to-cash workflows and revenue automation tools (e.g., NetSuite ARM, etc)

  • Show expert knowledge of consumption-based and subscription billing models with experience in complex pricing structures

  • Take a proactive, solutions-oriented approach to problem-solving in fast-paced, dynamic environments

Apply:

If you are passionate about solving complex revenue challenges in a dynamic AI environment and want to join a team that values technical excellence and innovation, we encourage you to apply.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

 - Partner with the Deal Desk, Go-to-Market, and Strategic Finance teams to standardize and automate deal approvals, optimizing revenue recognition while supporting sales initiatives

 - Execute complex ASC 606 technical evaluations and draft comprehensive revenue accounting memos

 - Assist with the external audit process and participate in and/or lead ad hoc projects with cross-functional impacts

You may be a good fit if you:

 - Have 6+ years of experience in ASC 606 for SaaS, consumption-based, and professional services arrangements

 - Have 6+ years of experience in internal controls, SOX/ICFR, audit, financial process risk, or financial operations within high-growth, tech, or SaaS environments

 - Have proven experience advising Product and GTM teams on revenue recognition implications

 - Demonstrate strong contract review experience with multi-element arrangements, milestone-based SOWs, and credit/usage models

 - Have familiarity with order-to-cash workflows and revenue automation tools (e.g., NetSuite ARM, etc)

 - Show expert knowledge of consumption-based and subscription billing models with experience in complex pricing structures

 - Take a proactive, solutions-oriented approach to problem-solving in fast-paced, dynamic environments

Apply:

If you are passionate about solving complex revenue challenges in a dynamic AI environment and want to join a team that values technical excellence and innovation, we encourage you to apply.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form https://docs.google.com/forms/d/12a6IrLdF3kI2nonKSr4tiFuz18rLQbaeYV-JM9L4o9Q/edit, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Research Internship (Fall / Winter 2026)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

To have the opportunity to collaborate with Cohere researchers and tools on designing and implementing novel research ideas and shipping state-of-the-art models to production. We have openings in teams covering base model training, retrieval augmented generation, data and evaluation, safety, and finetuning, to name a few; and we are open to receiving intern applications in any research area relating to LLMs to broaden your research connections while obtaining deep experience in a growing AI startup. 
Please Note: To be eligible for a Research Internship, you must be currently pursuing a PhD in Machine Learning, NLP, or a related discipline. You need to be available for a full-time internship that lasts for 4-6 months.

As a Cohere Research Intern, you will:

  • Conduct cutting-edge machine learning research, building and training large language models. 

  • Focus on research projects aimed at expanding the frontier of knowledge in language modelling and associate areas such as evaluation, multimodal models, optimisation etc.

  • Disseminate your research results through the production of publications, datasets, and code.

  • Contribute to research initiatives that have practical applications in Cohere’s product development. 

You may be a good fit if you:

  • Are currently pursuing, or in the process of obtaining, a PhD in Machine Learning, NLP, Artificial Intelligence, or a related discipline. We will also consider exceptional non-PhD candidates.

  • Are eligible for work authorization in the country of employment at the time of hire and maintain ongoing work authorization throughout the internship period. 

  • Have experience using large-scale distributed training strategies, data annotation and evaluation pipelines, or implementing state of the art ML models.

  • Are familiar with autoregressive sequence models, such as Transformers.

  • Have strong communication and problem-solving skills with the ability to convey complex research findings clearly and succinctly. 

  • Have knowledge, or are knowledgeable, of programming languages such as Python, C, C++, Lua, or related languages.

  • Have knowledge of related ML frameworks such as JAX, Pytorch and Tensorflow.

  • Have previous experience in building systems based on machine learning and deep learning techniques. 

  • Demonstrate passion for applied NLP models and products.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Demonstrated expertise through publications in top tier venues in fields such as machine learning, NLP, artificial intelligence, computer vision, optimization, computer science, statistics, applied mathematics, or data science. 

  • Proven ability to tackle analytical problems using quantitative methodologies. 

  • Proficiency in handling and analysing complex, high-dimensional data from various sources.

  • Experience in applying theoretical and empirical research to real-world problem-solving.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: HR Business Partner - Tech
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

Join Cohere as an HR Business Partner: a collaborative partner and a trusted advisor who will enable impact across our tech teams. In this role, you’ll help bridge our growth with our commitment to an exceptional employee experience. You’ll roll up your sleeves to support day-to-day operations while contributing to scalable solutions for the future.

In this individual contributor role, you will design and implement people operations processes while collaborating closely with tech leaders across the organization. We’re seeking someone who can bring fresh ideas and a passion for supporting teams as they scale. This role offers a unique blend of hands-on support and strategic contribution.

In this role, you will

  • Build strong relationships with tech leaders at all levels, from new managers to senior managers, providing guidance and support as they navigate growth.

  • Serve as a trusted advisor for talent strategy, people development, and organizational design within your tech client groups.

  • Support critical HR projects, including hiring, onboarding, performance management, and compensation, ensuring alignment with tech team needs.

  • Empower tech leaders to strengthen their leadership skills through feedback and actionable guidance.

  • Navigate ambiguity and solve people challenges, building solutions that work for teams today and as they grow.

  • Assist the SVP People with initiatives that support tech teams and global operations.

  • Leverage your understanding of employment laws to support tech teams in a global context.

This career opportunity may be a good match for you if you have:

  • 3-5 years of experience as an HR Business Partner or similar role in technology companies.

  • Experience supporting teams in fast-paced, growth-oriented environments.

  • Strong understanding of tech culture and experience partnering with Engineering and technical leaders.

  • A track record of building relationships, creating processes, and adapting to shifting priorities.

  • Experience resolving people issues with fairness and empathy, particularly in tech contexts.

  • A passion for enabling tech teams to thrive and scale effectively.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research

jobs.title: Manager, Total Rewards
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Role Overview

The Manager, Total Rewards will have the unique opportunity to build and scale the global total rewards program at Cohere. This senior level individual contributor role is critical to establishing a global compensation and benefits framework that aligns with our company’s values, growth objectives, and competitive positioning in the tech industry. You’ll lead the design, implementation, and optimization of total rewards programs that support a diverse, distributed workforce.

Key Responsibilities

  • Build the Function: Enhance, design and launch global total rewards programs (vision, strategy, and roadmap) and align them with Cohere’s global growth goals.

  • Global Compensation Strategy: Implement a competitive, equitable, and scalable compensation structure for all roles across regions, including pay and equity.

  • Benefits & Perks: Design and manage a comprehensive, globally consistent benefits package that meets the diverse needs of Cohere’s employees, including health, wellness, retirement, and unique perks.

  • Market Research & Benchmarking: Conduct regular market analysis to ensure Cohere’s total rewards offerings remain competitive and aligned with industry standards.

  • Compliance & Governance: Ensure all total rewards programs comply with local labour laws, regulations, and tax requirements across multiple jurisdictions.

  • Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with People, Finance, and leadership teams across the organization to align total rewards strategies with business objectives and budget constraints.

  • Communication & Education: Develop and deliver clear, engaging communications to employees about total rewards programs, ensuring transparency and understanding.

  • Data-Driven Decision Making: Use analytics to measure the effectiveness of total rewards initiatives and identify opportunities for improvement.

Qualifications

  • Experience: 10+ years in a total rewards role, with a proven track record of building and scaling total rewards functions at fast growing tech companies.

  • Global Expertise: Experience designing and managing total rewards programs for a global, distributed workforce across multiple regions.

  • Technical Skills: Strong analytical skills, proficiency in HRIS systems, and familiarity with industry leading compensation benchmarking tools.

  • Knowledge: Deep understanding of compensation philosophies, benefits regulations, and best practices in the tech industry.

  • Mindset: Strategic thinker with a passion for creating equitable, motivating, and scalable rewards programs. Alignment with Cohere’s values of innovation, collaboration, and integrity.

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, or a related field. Master’s degree or relevant certifications preferred.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

jobs.title: Manager, Learning and Development
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Role Overview

The Learning and Development Manager will have the unique opportunity to build the L&D function from scratch at Cohere. This senior level individual contributor role is critical to establishing a foundation for continuous learning and skill development as the company grows. You’ll design, implement, and scale programs that cultivate the skills, knowledge, and capabilities of Cohere’s employees, ensuring our team remains at the forefront of AI innovation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Build the Foundation: Create and launch the L&D function, including defining its vision, strategy, and roadmap aligned with Cohere’s growth objectives.

  • Program Design & Delivery: Develop and execute learning initiatives from the ground up, including technical training, leadership development, and soft skills programs.

  • Needs Assessment: Identify skill gaps and learning needs across the organization through surveys, interviews, and performance data to inform program development.

  • Content Creation: Curate and create high-quality learning materials, including workshops, e-learning modules, and resources tailored to Cohere’s unique context.

  • Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with leaders, managers, and teams to ensure learning programs are relevant, impactful, and integrated into daily workflows.

  • Evaluation & Improvement: Measure the effectiveness of learning initiatives using metrics like engagement, skill acquisition, and business impact, and iterate based on feedback.

  • Culture Building: Foster a culture of continuous learning by promoting growth mindsets, recognizing achievements, and encouraging knowledge sharing.

  • External Partnerships: Identify and collaborate with external vendors, thought leaders, or institutions to enhance Cohere’s learning offerings.

Qualifications

  • Experience: 7+ years in a senior level impactful role in learning and development, with a proven track record of building and scaling L&D functions in tech or AI-driven environments.

  • Skills: Strong project management, communication, and facilitation skills. Ability to translate complex technical concepts into accessible learning content.

  • Knowledge: Familiarity with adult learning principles, instructional design methodologies, and learning technologies (e.g., LMS platforms).

  • Mindset: Passion for empowering individuals and teams to reach their full potential. Alignment with Cohere’s values of innovation, collaboration, and integrity.

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Education, Organizational Development, or a related field. Master’s degree preferred.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

 - Program Design & Delivery: Develop and execute learning initiatives from the ground up, including technical training, leadership development, and soft skills programs.

 - Needs Assessment: Identify skill gaps and learning needs across the organization through surveys, interviews, and performance data to inform program development.

 - Content Creation: Curate and create high-quality learning materials, including workshops, e-learning modules, and resources tailored to Cohere’s unique context.

 - Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with leaders, managers, and teams to ensure learning programs are relevant, impactful, and integrated into daily workflows.

 - Evaluation & Improvement: Measure the effectiveness of learning initiatives using metrics like engagement, skill acquisition, and business impact, and iterate based on feedback.

 - Culture Building: Foster a culture of continuous learning by promoting growth mindsets, recognizing achievements, and encouraging knowledge sharing.

 - External Partnerships: Identify and collaborate with external vendors, thought leaders, or institutions to enhance Cohere’s learning offerings.

Qualifications

 - Experience: 7+ years in a senior level impactful role in learning and development, with a proven track record of building and scaling L&D functions in tech or AI-driven environments.

 - Skills: Strong project management, communication, and facilitation skills. Ability to translate complex technical concepts into accessible learning content.

 - Knowledge: Familiarity with adult learning principles, instructional design methodologies, and learning technologies (e.g., LMS platforms).

 - Mindset: Passion for empowering individuals and teams to reach their full potential. Alignment with Cohere’s values of innovation, collaboration, and integrity.

 - Education: Bachelor’s degree in Education, Organizational Development, or a related field. Master’s degree preferred.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form https://docs.google.com/forms/d/12a6IrLdF3kI2nonKSr4tiFuz18rLQbaeYV-JM9L4o9Q/edit, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Manager of Technical Staff, Sovereign AI
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

This role is hands-on and research-driven. You will design and implement novel research ideas, ship state of the art models to production, and maintain deep connections to academia and the government. You’ll dive into the latest literature on LLMs, experiment with frontier models, and lead a team of talented engineers and researchers to build scalable, production-ready solutions. At Cohere, we blend engineering and research seamlessly—everyone contributes to both, depending on their interests and organizational needs. We provide access to world-class compute resources, data, and talent to ensure you can do your best work.

Note: This role is in-person in Toronto only.

As the Manager for the Sovereign AI Modelling team, you will:

  • Manage a team of scientists and engineers, fostering a culture of high-performance, innovation and continuous learning

  • Stay up-to-date with the latest research in LLMs and related fields

  • Lead scalable strategies to train frontier models

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams across modelling, forward-deployed engineering, and solutions architecture

You may be a good fit if you have:

  • A PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field, with publications in top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, EMNLP)

  • Deep expertise in LLMs, with a strong understanding of the latest research and trends

  • Prior experience managing a team of engineers and/or scientists

  • Strong software engineering skills, with proficiency in Python and PyTorch, TensorFlow, or similar frameworks

  • Experience with distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, and scalable architectures

  • Excellent communication and organizational skills, with the ability to lead and motivate a team

  • A proactive, self-motivated mindset, with a passion for solving ambitious, open-ended problems

Canadian citizenship and eligibility for security clearance (required for this role).

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

jobs.title: Customer Marketing Manager
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

Cohere is seeking a creative and customer-centric Customer Marketing Manager to champion our customer advocacy efforts. In this pivotal role, you will collaborate on compelling customer stories and build marketing campaigns that highlight the value our customers derive from Cohere’s AI solutions. As the Customer Marketing Manager, you will play a key role in bridging the gap between our customers and the broader marketing organization. You will work closely with the sales, customer success and product teams to identify, develop, and share impactful customer narratives, events, solutions and content that resonate with our target audience.

In this role, you will collaborate extensively with cross-functional teams to ensure our customer stories are integrated into all aspects of our marketing strategy. Your ability to create and execute customer-focused campaigns will be crucial in driving customer retention, advocacy, and growth.

What you’ll do:

  • Engage with key customers to uncover success stories that demonstrate the impact of Cohere’s AI solutions.

  • Champion our customers by supporting content pieces that are woven into blogs, social media, website content, whitepapers, sales enablement and other marketing materials.

  • Create unique events alongside the events team to support customers with education, enablement and empowerment.

  • Source speaking opportunities that align with our customers’ goals, driving brand awareness and demonstrating the impact of AI for our customers

  • Craft compelling case studies, testimonials, and customer narratives that highlight customer challenges, solutions, and outcomes.

  • Design and execute marketing campaigns centred around customer stories to drive awareness, engagement, and advocacy.

  • Partner with the customer success, product, sales, marketing and events teams to align on go-to-market strategies to drive our customer advocacy narrative.

  • Track and analyze the performance of customer-focused marketing campaigns, using insights to optimize future efforts.

  • Develop and maintain dashboards to monitor key metrics related to customer engagement and advocacy.

This career opportunity may be a good match for you if you have:

  • 7+ years of marketing experience in a technology-focused company, with at least 3 years of experience customer marketing for a high-growth company

  • Exceptional storytelling and writing skills, with the ability to craft compelling narratives that resonate with diverse audiences.

  • Proven experience in building and executing marketing campaigns that drive customer engagement and advocacy.

  • Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and collaborate effectively across teams.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships with customers and internal stakeholders.

  • Experience with marketing tools and platforms, such as HubSpot, Salesforce, and Google Analytics, is a plus.

  • A passion for AI and technology, with a keen interest in understanding how AI can drive business outcomes.

Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Data Science, or a related field. An advanced degree is a plus

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Data Engineer
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

We're building the foundational infrastructure that will define how the world thinks about and deploys AI, and we want the sharpest, most curious people to help us do it. As a member of our Analytics & Data Insights team, you'll tackle the kind of problems that don't have textbook answers yet, launch products that didn't exist a year ago, and help enterprises understand what foundational AI actually means for their bottom line.

As a Data Engineer, you will:

  • Work directly on storage infrastructure, product launches, and new customer experiences built on one of the most advanced AI systems in the world

  • Collaborate daily with researchers and engineers who are some of the best in the world at what they do

  • Run implementations end-to-end and see initiatives through to real outcomes — no waiting around to be told what to do

  • Partner across research, marketing, sales, and finance to help define how Cohere grows, with your recommendations feeding directly into products and strategy

You may be a good fit if you have:

  • 5+ years of experience working on production-grade data processing systems

  • Strong command of Python and SQL

  • Experience with distributed data processing frameworks such as Apache Beam, Spark, or Flink

  • The ability to transform unstructured data into performant datasets across diverse storage backends including S3, GCS, and POSIX

  • Experience with modern orchestration platforms, especially Kubernetes (nice to have)

  • Familiarity with modern analytics stack tooling such as BigQuery, Airflow, or dbt (nice to have)

  • Knowledge of Java or Golang (nice to have)

  • Genuine excitement about AI - you follow the research, have opinions, and enjoy being in the weeds

  • Comfort operating at the edge of what's known, with a desire to build something genuinely new rather than optimise what already exists

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, FDE Infrastructure (EMEA)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

About the Role

We're looking for an Engineering Manager to lead our Deployment Engineering team in EMEA. This isn't a typical management role — we need someone who leads from the front, gets their hands dirty, and drives impact. You'll manage a team of Forward Deployed Engineers who are on the front lines of deploying Cohere's North platform into customer environments. You should be ready to be a force to be reckoned with.

Location: EMEA (remote-first)

What You'll Do

  • Lead and mentor a team of Forward Deployed Engineers across EMEA

  • Drive end-to-end deployment of North in private cloud and on-premises environments

  • Take ownership of customer success from technical implementation through delivery

  • Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and Sales to shape how we deliver AI to enterprises

  • Mentor your team on cloud infrastructure, Kubernetes, and enterprise-grade deployments

  • Optimize performance for OpenSearch, databases, and other K8s services

  • Define scaling guidelines for GPU and CPU compute resources

  • Build processes & technology that scales — we're growing fast.

What We're Looking For

  • 5+ years of experience in software engineering with demonstrated leadership

  • Hands-on experience deploying enterprise software at scale

  • Strong expertise in cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, GCP)

  • Experience with Kubernetes, Helm, and CI/CD pipelines

  • High agency — you don't wait for permission to solve problems

  • A doer mentality — you dig in and get stuff done

  • Experience managing engineers in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

  • Excellent communication skills in English.

Nice to Have's

  • Fluency in additional European languages

  • Experience with AI/ML infrastructure

  • Background in enterprise security and compliance.

What We Offer

  • Remote-flexible work from anywhere in EMEA

  • 6 weeks of paid vacation

  • Equity/stock options

  • Competitive salary

  • Co-working stipend

  • Annual learning allowance ($2,000)

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

 - Experience with Kubernetes, Helm, and CI/CD pipelines

 - High agency — you don't wait for permission to solve problems

 - A doer mentality — you dig in and get stuff done

 - Experience managing engineers in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

 - Excellent communication skills in English.


NICE TO HAVE'S

 - Fluency in additional European languages

 - Experience with AI/ML infrastructure

 - Background in enterprise security and compliance.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Remote-flexible work from anywhere in EMEA

 - 6 weeks of paid vacation

 - Equity/stock options

 - Competitive salary

 - Co-working stipend

 - Annual learning allowance ($2,000)

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. jobs.title: Security Officer - Sovereign AI
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

We are seeking a Security Officer to oversee and implement security protocols in alignment with Canadian industrial security standards. The Security Officer will play a vital role in overseeing Cohere's continued commitment to meet the security requirements of federal government contracts in Canada. This role will also act as a liaison with Public Services and Procurement Canada (PSPC).

As a Security Officer, you will:

  • Act as official point of contact with Public Services and Procurement Canada's (PSPC) Contract Security Program (CSP).

  • Ensure compliance with security policies, procedures, and regulations for handling classified information aligned with PSPC and Contract Security Program (CSP) requirements.

  • Oversee security screening processes for employees, contractors, and visitors, including background checks and reliability/secret clearance coordination.

  • Enforce access controls, secure storage, and transmission protocols for classified materials.

  • Report security incidents to relevant authorities (e.g., CSP, Canadian Security Intelligence Service) as required.

  • Maintain accurate security records, including personnel clearances, training logs, and incident reports.

  • Manage security briefings, debriefings, and ongoing security awareness training for cleared personnel.

  • Liaise with government agencies during audits or compliance reviews.

  • Ensure security requirements in government contracts are met, including CSP clauses.

  • Collaborate with procurement and legal teams to address security-related contractual obligations.

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Mandatory: Canadian citizenship and current residency in Canada .

  • Experience: 5+ years in security operations, with prior experience in industrial/government security preferred.

  • Clearance: Valid security clearance (Top Secret or higher preferred) and familiarity with CSP/PSPC frameworks.

Application Note: Only candidates meeting the citizenship and residency requirements will be considered. Candidates in Ottawa or within commuting distance will be strongly preferred.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Summer HR Intern – Rippling HRIS Implementation
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

This internship offers a unique opportunity to make a tangible impact on Cohere’s HR operations while developing skills in data management, system implementation, and cross-functional collaboration. As an HR Data Intern, you’ll play a critical role in ensuring the success of our Rippling HRIS implementation—a project that will streamline processes, improve data accuracy, and enhance the employee experience across the organization.

Why This Role Stands Out

  • Real-World Impact: Your work will directly contribute to a major organizational initiative, ensuring HR data is accurate, structured, and ready for the new system.

  • Skill Development: Gain hands-on experience with HRIS systems, data validation, and documentation—skills highly transferable across industries and roles.

  • Mentorship & Collaboration: Work closely with HR and IT professionals, learning from their expertise and building valuable connections within the company.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in auditing and cleaning existing HR data to ensure accuracy and consistency.

  • Help structure and document HR processes and workflows for Rippling.

  • Collaborate with HR and IT teams to identify and resolve data discrepancies.

  • Support testing and validation of the Rippling system post-implementation.

Qualifications

  • Strong attention to detail and ability to work with large datasets.

  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines without compromising on quality.

  • Experience with data organization, documentation, or structured workflows.

  • A degree in Information Science, Library Science, HR, or a related field (preferred but not required).

  • Familiarity with HR processes or HRIS systems (preferred but not required).

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Part-time Executive Assistant (Contract)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

We are looking for a solutions-driven, detail-oriented, organised Executive Assistant to support our leadership team members in Korea. Reporting to the Manager of Administration, this person will have the opportunity to work cross-functionally and provide high value and impact to the leadership team and the company as a whole. If you are someone that thrives in a dynamic, collaborative, and fast-paced environment and are interested in joining a company that’s in growth mode, we’d be delighted to hear from you!

Please Note:

This is a part-time role, 20 hours/week. Candidates must be based in Korea and must possess Native-level Korean & fluent business-level English communication skills.

As an Executive Assistant, you will:

  • Manage the day-to-day schedules for our leadership team, including calendaring, booking meetings and other commitments (where necessary) to ensure a balanced and efficient workday

  • Coordinate internal and external meetings (think 1:1s, board meetings, partner meetings) and assist with documentation that may be required in conjunction with meetings

  • Arrange international/domestic travel and logistics for our leadership team, including booking flights and accommodations.

  • Be willing to take on special projects like coordinating team events, off sites or community-building activities

  • Communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders while maintaining a strict level of confidentiality

  • Create and maintain internal documentation, share knowledge, and also work on formatting communications for the leadership team

You may be a good fit if you:

  • Have 2+ years of experience working as an Executive Assistant, Administrative Assistant, or equivalent experience

  • Anticipate others’ needs and can work proactively to address them

  • Have previous experience partnering with senior leaders in a fast-paced, growing, innovative environment

  • Have experience managing multiple calendars, demonstrating flexibility and problem-solving abilities to adjust or prioritize schedules

  • Are a strong communicator, both written and verbal - and feel comfortable speaking to C-level stakeholders

  • Are highly self-motivated and exercise discretion, tact, and professionalism

  • Are detail-oriented, love staying organized, and stay cool under tight deadlines

  • Are experienced with tools like Slack, Google Calendar, Google Sheets, Google Slides

  • Enjoy a balance of collaborative and independent work

  • Have experience creating and maintaining positive working relationships with internal and external stakeholders

*Please note that below-listed benefits are not included for this contract role

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Data Annotation Specialist, Engineering
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

This role will focus on breaking down engineering tasks such as reporting, component search and handling of material billing/information into individual steps that can be tackled through tool use. Your breakdown will teach the Cohere model the logic needed to complete each task.

Please Note: This is a part-time independent contractor position available within Canada. We seek candidates who are able to commit to 16 hours per week minimum at a 30 CAD/hour contract rate. This role is BYOD 💻 - Bring Your Own Device (laptop). Remote work within Canada. 12 month contract. Performance incentives included!

As a Data Annotation Specialist, you will:

  • Evaluate the model's ability to respond to engineering procedures and workflows

  • Task models to complete engineering tasks along with verified information to evaluate the accuracy of the model's responses

  • Label, proofread, and improve machine-written and human-written engineering-related outputs.

  • Follow our style guide, and make recommendations on unique situations that fall outside of its scope.

You may be a good fit if you have:

  • 3+ years of industry experience working in engineering fields (civil, mechanical, electrical, chemical

  • Technician/engineering specialist training and experience preferredAn education background in engineering (Bachelors, Masters or similar)

  • Experience on medium-large scale engineering projects involving designing, prototyping and manufacturing components

  • Experience with CAD software, material billing and product lifecycle management (PCMs)

The Candidate Journey:
Initial Screening - Once you have submitted your application our Talent Team will review your resume and writing samples.
Virtual Annotation Test - This assignment will test your written skill through various language-based tasks, such as a a writing sample, interacting with a chat bot, and more.
Video Screen - If selected to move forward, you will have a short video call with a member of our Operations Team!
Offer - Independent Contractor Agreement


As an independent contractor, you maintain control over how you complete your work and may work with multiple clients simultaneously, although we ask you to declare if any of these are with a direct competitor of Cohere and maintain IP confidentiality of the Cohere project. Independent contractors are not eligible for health benefits or other benefits provided to employees. Compensation for services is provided to contractors by contractors invoicing for services provided pursuant to the terms of our agreement with the contractor.

It is important to understand that as an independent contractor, continuous work is not guaranteed. The client-contractor relationship is fundamentally project-based, meaning engagements may be temporary, periodic, or intermittent based on our organizational needs and project availability. As an independent contractor, you should anticipate fluctuations in workflow and therefore compensation for services when Cohere does not require as many hours of services in a week.

Prospective candidates, please be advised: this role involves working with human generated and model generated tasks that may involve exposure to not safe for work (NSFW) text content as part of data annotation tasks, including explicit, offensive, or other inappropriate material.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

Prospective candidates, please be advised: this role involves working with human generated and model generated tasks that may involve exposure to not safe for work (NSFW) text content as part of data annotation tasks, including explicit, offensive, or other inappropriate material.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form https://docs.google.com/forms/d/12a6IrLdF3kI2nonKSr4tiFuz18rLQbaeYV-JM9L4o9Q/edit, and we will work together to meet your needs.

jobs.title: Research Internship Reinforcement Learning (Summer)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Duration: Minimum 4 months (summer 2026, with potential extension)

About the Project

This internship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge research in reinforcement learning (RL) and large language models (LLMs), focusing on two interconnected projects:

  1. Combining Self-Distillation and Reinforcement Learning for LLMs, with Applications to Code and Agentic Tasks
     This project explores how LLMs can improve through self-reflection and iterative learning by combining reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) and self-distillation. The focus is on scenarios where structured feedback from verifiers, compilers, unit tests, or tool calls enables models to detect errors, revise outputs, and learn from failures. The internship will bridge theoretical mathematical modeling of self-distillation with practical, production-oriented implementation.

  2. Dealing with Extremely Large Rollouts in RLVR
     As RLVR becomes a cornerstone for training reasoning-oriented LLMs, the challenge of handling extremely large rollouts grows. This project investigates mechanisms such as summarization, memory, context compaction, hierarchical sub-agents, and resumable rollouts to enable unbounded or very long trajectories. It also explores how to effectively learn from such trajectories, as traditional RLVR objectives fail when episodes exceed context window limits.

Both projects are grounded in recent research and aim to advance the state-of-the-art in LLM training and deployment.

Responsibilities

  • Conduct literature reviews and implement state-of-the-art algorithms in RL and self-distillation.

  • Design and execute experiments to evaluate the effectiveness of proposed methods on code generation and agentic tasks.

  • Develop and maintain codebases for both theoretical modeling and practical implementations.

  • Collaborate with researchers to analyze results, refine methodologies, and prepare findings for publication.

  • Contribute to the design of mechanisms for handling large rollouts, such as summarization and hierarchical sub-agents.

  • Document progress, methodologies, and outcomes clearly and comprehensively.

Requirements

  • Technical Skills:

    • Strong background in machine learning, particularly reinforcement learning and deep learning.

    • Proficiency in Python and experience with ML frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow).

    • Familiarity with LLMs and their training paradigms.

    • Experience with coding tasks, unit testing, or compiler tools is a plus.

  • Educational Background:

    • Currently pursuing a Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field.

  • Soft Skills:

    • Ability to work independently and manage complex projects.

    • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.

    • Excellent communication skills for collaborating with a research team.

  • Additional:

    • Prior experience with RLVR, self-distillation, or large-scale ML experiments is highly desirable.

    • Willingness to learn and adapt to new methodologies and tools.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Lead Member of Technical Staff, Inference Infrastructure
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

Are you energized by leading the design of high-performance, scalable and reliable machine learning systems? Do you want to set technical direction and help shape the next generation of AI platforms powering advanced NLP applications? We are looking for a Lead Member of Technical Staff to join the Model Serving team at Cohere. The team is responsible for developing, deploying, and operating the AI platform delivering Cohere's large language models through easy to use API endpoints. In this role, you will provide technical leadership across multiple teams, driving the architecture and strategy for deploying optimized NLP models to production in low latency, high throughput, and high availability environments. You will serve as a key point of contact for customers, leading the design of customized deployments to meet their specific needs, and mentoring engineers to raise the technical bar across the team.

You may be a good fit if you have:

  • 8+ years of engineering experience running production infrastructure at a large scale, with a track record of technical leadership

  • Demonstrated experience leading the architecture and design of large, highly available distributed systems with Kubernetes and GPU workloads on those clusters

  • Deep expertise with Kubernetes dev and production coding and support, including setting team-wide standards and best practices

  • Extensive experience across GCP, Azure, AWS, OCI, and multi-cloud on-prem / hybrid serving environments, with the ability to guide strategic infrastructure decisions

  • Proven ability to lead the design, deployment, support, and troubleshooting of complex Linux-based computing environments at scale

  • Experience owning compute/storage/network resource and cost management at an organisational level, including optimisation strategies

  • Exceptional collaboration and communication skills, with experience mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional initiatives to build mission-critical systems

  • The grit and adaptability to both solve and guide others through complex technical challenges that evolve day to day

  • Strong expertise in the computational characteristics of accelerators (GPUs, TPUs, and/or custom accelerators), and how to leverage them to drive latency and throughput improvements at scale

  • Deep knowledge of distributed systems, with experience establishing patterns and practices across engineering teams

  • Proficiency in Golang, C++ or other languages designed for high-performance scalable servers, with the ability to set coding standards and conduct senior-level technical reviews

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We are expanding our Talent Team and are looking for a creative, high-performing Technical Recruiter eager to take on a new challenge.

In this role, you will own full cycle recruitment efforts across our tech teams, including Research, Applied ML, and Engineering.

As a Talent Partner at Cohere, you will:

  • Own a world class full lifecycle recruitment process, attracting and recruiting top talent for a large volume of senior level roles across engineering teams.

  • Partner with hiring managers and org leaders on effective recruiting strategies. Influence where necessary.

  • Manage recruiting funnels in Ashby, with diligence.

  • Proactively source and recruit top talent.

  • Take initiative, introducing talent projects in an effort to optimize efficiencies and take ownership of these projects.

This career opportunity may be a good match for you if you have:

  • 7+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience at high growth technology companies with a focus on delivering top talent for Research, MLE and Engineering teams.

  • Experience managing a high volume senior level desk at a hyper growth startup/scale-up.

  • Creative problem solving skills & thrive under pressure.

  • Strong ability to excel in rapidly evolving ambiguous environments.

  • You are a natural entrepreneur, an influencer, and enjoy working cross-functionally.
