jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer, Infrastructure Specialist (Public Sector)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

About North:

North is Cohere's cutting-edge AI workspace platform, designed to revolutionize the way enterprises utilize AI. It offers a secure and customizable environment, allowing companies to deploy AI while maintaining control over sensitive data. North integrates seamlessly with existing workflows, providing a trusted platform that connects AI agents with workplace tools and applications.

Why This Role?

Cohere’s team partners with Canadian public sector organisations to unlock transformative value through secure, ethical deployment of Generative AI (GenAI) solutions. We work collaboratively to address complex societal challenges while maintaining the highest standards of data security and compliance. You will work directly with public sector customers to quickly understand their greatest problems and design and implement solutions using Cohere's stack.

This role offers a unique opportunity to shape how enterprises harness the power of AI in real-world applications. As a bridge between our core North product and our clients’ engineering teams, you’ll be at the forefront of solving complex problems and securely integrating AI into critical sectors.

We are seeking engineers with diverse skill sets, including backend, infrastructure, agent development, and deployments, who deeply care about customers and want to work at the cutting edge of Agentic AI.

Location: Ottawa, 20-40% travel anticipated.


Security Clearance: Active Top Secret clearance strongly preferred; candidates eligible and willing to obtain clearance will also be considered.

More information about Canadian Security Clearance can be found here.

In this role, you will:

  • Lead end-to-end deployment of North in private cloud and on-premises environments, including planning, configuration, testing, and rollout.

  • Partner with enterprise IT teams to assess infrastructure, security requirements, and data management practices.

  • Experiment at a high velocity and with a high level of quality to engage our customers and ultimately deliver solutions that exceed their expectations

  • Design and implement deployment strategies tailored to client needs, ensuring compliance with data privacy and security standards.

  • Troubleshoot and resolve deployment-related technical issues, providing timely solutions to minimize downtime.

You may be a good fit if:

  • You have experience with and enjoy working directly with customers

  • You have experience deploying enterprise software in private/hybrid cloud environments

  • You have proven experience administering production Kubernetes clusters and expertise with Helm

  • Familiarity with DevOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and tools like Git for version control

  • You have strong expertise in cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, GCP), networking, and virtualization

  • You excel in fast-paced environments and can execute while priorities and objectives are a moving target

  • Familiarity with Canadian public sector security and compliance requirements (e.g., data sovereignty, access controls).

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

 - Troubleshoot and resolve deployment-related technical issues, providing timely solutions to minimize downtime.

You may be a good fit if:

 - You have experience with and enjoy working directly with customers

 - You have experience deploying enterprise software in private/hybrid cloud environments

 - You have proven experience administering production Kubernetes clusters and expertise with Helm

 - Familiarity with DevOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and tools like Git for version control

 - You have strong expertise in cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS, GCP), networking, and virtualization

 - You excel in fast-paced environments and can execute while priorities and objectives are a moving target

 - Familiarity with Canadian public sector security and compliance requirements (e.g., data sovereignty, access controls).

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form https://docs.google.com/forms/d/12a6IrLdF3kI2nonKSr4tiFuz18rLQbaeYV-JM9L4o9Q/edit, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Recruitment Coordinator
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

Cohere is in a unique and exciting position to grow our technical team globally! We are expanding our Talent Team and are looking for creative, high-performing Talent Coordinators who are eager to take on a new challenge as we are in the early stages of building the Talent Acquisition function. In this role, you will master a new domain as a Talent coordinator, and influence the scaling of a high-growth Canadian tech company. If you consider yourself resilient, collaborative, influential, and a lifelong learner, we’d love to hear from you!

Please Note: We have offices in Toronto, San Francisco, and London but embrace being remote-first!

As a Talent Coordinator, you will:

  • Be a natural liaison between teams: It’s essential to build solid partnerships with those you’ll support, including hiring managers, the Talent Team and the rest of our People Ops function.

  • Manage the candidate experience: Build strong relationships with each hire and manage their interview process with seamless communication from start to finish. You are their primary point of contact so giving each candidate a world-class experience is not only important, it's an expectation of this role.

  • Schedule + prep interviews: Ensuring our hiring teams and candidates are 100% prepared to show up as their best selves.

  • Support with early talent & intern hiring: From updating/posting internship postings, to scheduling interviews, to helping at Hackathon/internship events when needed.

  • Help to optimize our interview process & ensure it promotes a positive candidate experience.

  • Serve as the liaison between the hiring managers and the candidates to provide status updates, answer questions, and ensure timely follow up.

  • Help ensuring that our internal documentation is kept up to date, working with detail and organization in all of our tools - Lever, Notion, Slack, etc.

  • Ensure data integrity and accuracy by maintaining the applicant tracking system.

You may be a good fit if:

  • High attention to detail - nothing gets by you, especially when it comes to communication with candidates.

  • You are a strong collaborator & team player, and can partner with a variety of functions and levels across the org.

  • You have great time management & communication skills - you know how long things can take and plan accordingly as you are dealing with multiple calendars, schedules, and ever-changing priorities.

  • You have experience using applicant tracking systems (e.g., Lever, Greenhouse) and have working knowledge of LinkedIn Recruiter.

  • You like to work in a fast-paced work environment, and execute tasks thoughtfully and efficiently. You take great pride in the role that you plan in cultivating strong candidate experiences and strive to give each candidate a high-touch, positive experience with Cohere.

  • Ability to handle confidential and sensitive information with tact and discretion is critical and expected.

  • You are excited to learn more about our business and the AI industry and share this same passion with our candidates.

  • You are agile and work well under pressure.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Senior Finance Manager
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

About this role:

In this high-impact role, you will sit at the intersection of Finance, Sales, Marketing, and Partnerships. You will use your strong finance acumen and leadership skills to influence revenue plans and annual quotas targets, structure deals and strategic partnerships and optimize Cohere’s customer acquisition costs. The ideal candidate will be a seasoned leader who can blend traditional financial rigor with the agility and foresight required in a high-growth environment. You will own and drive business-critical analyses, and prepare Cohere for growth and scale.

Financial Planning & Revenue Strategy

  • Lead the annual and quarterly planning process for revenue, ensuring alignment between GTM targets and company-wide financial goals.

  • In close partnership with Sales Leadership, design sales quota allocation across regions and develop the perspective on capacity planning (i.e. how many reps Cohere needs to hire as it scales?)

  • Establish the ROI framework for GTM investments, performing rigorous post-mortem analyses on high-spend initiatives - such as major marketing events or campaigns - to quantify performance against expected returns and inform future capital allocation.

Strategic Partnership & Analysis:

  • Lead the financial evaluation and structuring of all major commercial deals and strategic partnerships; connect the dots between cross-functional stakeholders to drive success outcomes.

  • Collaborate with the Sales, Business Development and Legal teams to define financial terms, performance milestones, and payout structures that align partner incentives with the company's long-term financial goals.

  • Develop robust financial models to forecast the revenue, cost, profitability and ROI of new and existing deals, quantifying their impact on key metrics like ARR and gross margin.

  • Monitor and track the financial performance of commercial deals post-execution, ensuring compliance with contractual financial obligations and contributing to annual partner performance reviews.

This career opportunity may be a good match for you if you have:

  • 12+ years of experience in an FP&A, Corporate Finance or Strategic Finance within public and/or high-growth private companies, or a combination of education and experience

  • Proven leadership experience preferred

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, including effective communication skills at all levels within the Company and the ability to collaborate across functions.

  • Proven ability to deliver change, improve and streamline processes, drive efficiency, and influence stakeholders.

  • Ability to thrive in fast-paced, constantly involving environments. Startup/scaleup experience is a plus.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)

jobs.title: Finance PMO (Project Management Office)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

About this role:

We are seeking a high-caliber leader to stand up a top-notch Finance PMO (Project Management Office). This is a high-visibility, high-impact role designed for a strategic operator who thrives in the "gray space." You will be the glue between Finance, Legal, Engineering and Product, ensuring our most critical corporate milestones are executed with precision.

As our head of Finance PMO you will manage:

M&A Project Management Office

  • Establish the ‘M&A Playbook’ for the company, covering everything from initial due diligence to post-merger integration.

  • Functional Due Diligence: Coordinate across Engineering, HR, Legal, and Finance to identify risks and synergies during the deal phase.

  • Integration Lead: Drive the 100-day integration plan for newly acquired entities, ensuring culture, systems, and financial reporting are unified seamlessly.

Strategic Planning Cycle Management

  • AOP Orchestration: Architect and lead the end-to-end Annual Operating Plan (AOP) process, including the design of integrated planning timelines, standardized financial templates, and executive stage-gate reviews to ensure organizational alignment on fiscal goals.

  • Resource Capacity Validation: Drive strategic resource planning by performing gap analyses between proposed Product/Engineering roadmaps and current headcount constraints, ensuring the company has the necessary headcount to execute its highest-priority initiatives.

Special Projects

  • Act as the "special ops" lead for finance leadership on high-priority projects that don't have a natural home.

  • Process Optimization: Identify bottlenecks in our current financial operations and design scalable, automated solutions.

This career opportunity may be a good match for you if you have:

  • 8-10+ year progressive work experience Management Consulting, Strategic Finance/Planning, or a PMO lead role within a high-growth AI or SaaS environment

  • Experience with M&A cycles preferred (from DD to integration)

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, including effective communication skills at all levels within the company and the ability to collaborate across functions.

  • Proven ability to deliver change, improve and streamline processes, drive efficiency, and influence stakeholders.

  • Ability to thrive in fast-paced, constantly involving environments. Startup/scaleup experience is a plus.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

jobs.title: Manager, Real Estate & Facilities
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

About the Role

We’re seeking an experienced Manager, Real Estate & Facilities to join our team at Cohere. This position is perfect for someone who wants to take over a comprehensive operational portfolio that combines strategic real estate management with hands-on facilities oversight. You’ll be the point person for our global broker relationship (JLL), lease negotiations, and overall workplace strategy & operations.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Real Estate Management

  • Lead all lease negotiations, renewals, and terminations for our global portfolio

  • Manage and optimize our relationship with JLL as our global brokerage partner

  • Align headcount vs. office space strategy with FP&A and HR on budget parameters, ensuring offices are managed accordingly

  • Conduct market analysis and site selection in conjunction with the Head of Procurement, HR and Leadership for new locations and expansions

  • Oversee portfolio performance metrics and financial reporting

  • Manage real estate budget for the company

Facilities Operations & Vendor Management

Vendor Management & Negotiation

  • Negotiate contracts with vendors for cleaning, security, maintenance, and other facility services

  • Manage vendor relationships (including JLL as a key vendor partner), performance evaluations, and continuous improvement initiatives

Operations, Compliance & Implementation

  • Consolidate and streamline FM services across all office locations

  • Direct physical security operations and compliance across all sites

  • Develop and implement facilities policies, procedures, and sustainability initiatives

  • Manage facility budgets, capital planning, and expense optimization

  • Oversee facility moves, reconfigurations, and workplace experience enhancements

  • Ensure compliance with building codes, safety regulations, and lease agreements

This role may require occasional travel to visit office locations and conduct operational reviews.

Qualifications & Experience

Required

  • Experience: 8-10 years in combined real estate and facilities management

  • Broker/Vendor Management: Demonstrated success managing relationships with global brokerage firms and vendors

  • Lease Expertise: Strong background in commercial lease negotiation and administration

  • Strategic Thinking: Ability to link operational decisions to business strategy and growth objectives

  • Financial Partnership: Experience managing budgets in partnership with FP&A to ensure budget control and optimization

Preferred

  • Multi-site Portfolio: Experience managing real estate/facilities across multiple locations

  • CRE/FM Certifications: CPM, CFM, or similar professional designations

  • Technology Proficiency: Experience with FM software, lease management systems, and data analytics

Why Join Cohere

At Cohere, we’re building the future of AI while creating exceptional workplaces for our team. You’ll have:

  • Impact: Direct ownership of critical operational functions that support our global workforce

  • Growth: Opportunity to shape our real estate and workplace strategy as we scale

  • Innovation: Work with cutting-edge technology in a dynamic AI company

  • Culture: Collaborative environment where operational excellence is valued

  • Benefits: Comprehensive health, dental, vision, 401(k), and competitive compensation

Apply Now

Ready to take on a more strategic role? We want to hear from you if you have the experience and drive to manage our real estate and facilities operations at a high level.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)
jobs.descriptionPlain:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re seeking an experienced Manager, Real Estate & Facilities to join our team at Cohere. This position is perfect for someone who wants to take over a comprehensive operational portfolio that combines strategic real estate management with hands-on facilities oversight. You’ll be the point person for our global broker relationship (JLL), lease negotiations, and overall workplace strategy & operations.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



STRATEGIC REAL ESTATE MANAGEMENT

 - Lead all lease negotiations, renewals, and terminations for our global portfolio

 - Manage and optimize our relationship with JLL as our global brokerage partner

 - Align headcount vs. office space strategy with FP&A and HR on budget parameters, ensuring offices are managed accordingly

 - Conduct market analysis and site selection in conjunction with the Head of Procurement, HR and Leadership for new locations and expansions

 - Oversee portfolio performance metrics and financial reporting

 - Manage real estate budget for the company
   


FACILITIES OPERATIONS & VENDOR MANAGEMENT

Vendor Management & Negotiation

 - Negotiate contracts with vendors for cleaning, security, maintenance, and other facility services

 - Manage vendor relationships (including JLL as a key vendor partner), performance evaluations, and continuous improvement initiatives
   

Operations, Compliance & Implementation

 - Consolidate and streamline FM services across all office locations

 - Direct physical security operations and compliance across all sites

 - Develop and implement facilities policies, procedures, and sustainability initiatives

 - Manage facility budgets, capital planning, and expense optimization

 - Oversee facility moves, reconfigurations, and workplace experience enhancements

 - Ensure compliance with building codes, safety regulations, and lease agreements
   

This role may require occasional travel to visit office locations and conduct operational reviews.



QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE



REQUIRED

 - Experience: 8-10 years in combined real estate and facilities management

 - Broker/Vendor Management: Demonstrated success managing relationships with global brokerage firms and vendors

 - Lease Expertise: Strong background in commercial lease negotiation and administration

 - Strategic Thinking: Ability to link operational decisions to business strategy and growth objectives

 - Financial Partnership: Experience managing budgets in partnership with FP&A to ensure budget control and optimization


PREFERRED

 - Multi-site Portfolio: Experience managing real estate/facilities across multiple locations

 - CRE/FM Certifications: CPM, CFM, or similar professional designations

 - Technology Proficiency: Experience with FM software, lease management systems, and data analytics
   


WHY JOIN COHERE

At Cohere, we’re building the future of AI while creating exceptional workplaces for our team. You’ll have:

 - Impact: Direct ownership of critical operational functions that support our global workforce

 - Culture: Collaborative environment where operational excellence is valued

 - Benefits: Comprehensive health, dental, vision, 401(k), and competitive compensation
   


APPLY NOW
